description: Template for observer-led manual-fix work split across parallel agents. Observer instantiates this template into backlog chain_trigger_json before delegation.

observer_contract.exception_policy: Observer implements, waits, merges, pushes, or performs privileged transitions only when the user explicitly asks or a documented governance transition requires it.

runtime_identity.description: Observer must instantiate these values from durable task metadata before delegating work. Subagents must echo them in implementation and verification evidence.

local_precheck.description: Observer must run and record this dispatch gate before spawn_agent. Passing evidence proves the local mf_sub worker has an isolated branch/worktree/file fence before non-blocking handoff.

override_policy.description: Dispatch into the target/main worktree is blocked by default. A same-worktree exception must be operator-approved, exact dirty-scope checked, and recorded to timeline before spawn_agent.

graph_queries.description: Any subagent graph lookup must use audited query_source=mf_subagent with task identity and fence context; returned trace ids must be recorded in timeline evidence.

self_precheck.description: Observer must include the local self-precheck or gate precheck command/API expected for machine-consumed output, and the subagent must record compact self_check evidence.

gate_alignment.description: Observer must align required evidence ids, focused tests, E2E decision, and finish-gate expectations before delegation.

final_output.description: Worker must stop with structured review evidence and must not merge, push, mutate merge queues, close backlog, activate graph refs, release gates, or delete worktrees.

merge_review_checklist.description: Candidate output satisfies the instantiated worker contract and stops in an allowed review state.
Changed files stay within the assigned branch/worktree/file fence and match the owned-file list.
Focused tests and required E2E evidence are present, passing, or explicitly deferred with a follow-up backlog row.
Docs, test, config, fixture, and generated artifact impact is declared and reviewed.
Generated assets are either source-of-truth updates, reproducible outputs, or excluded from review with a reason.
Graph binding, Asset Inbox/Governance Hint needs, and post-merge reconcile/update plan are recorded.
Expected Chain trailers and explicit commit file list are ready before merge.
Backlog close is deferred until merge, verification, reconcile/update, and close-ready evidence are complete.

evidence_requirements.description: Observer records the test-scenario decision, reason, required evidence ids, and E2E run/defer/not-applicable decision before delegation.
Worker or observer records changed files, scope, and implementation notes.
Focused unit/API/typecheck evidence for each changed surface.
Build or compile evidence when frontend/runtime assets are affected.
Observer sets required=true when the instantiated work affects UI/API/operator flows or any E2E-covered surface.
Focused tests for the instantiated contract gate behavior when the work changes contract validation or close-gate policy.
Documentation update evidence when the work changes MF/subagent policy.
Follow-up backlog evidence when observer explicitly defers E2E.

gate_contract.waiver_policy: Required E2E can be disabled only in the instantiated contract with a reason and follow-up backlog for user-visible or API behavior.
